title: Spelt from Sibyl's Leaves
content:
آ آ آ آ Earnest, earthless, equal, attuneable, | vaulty, voluminous, . . stupendous
آ آ آ آ Evening strains to be tï؟½me's vï؟½st, | womb-of-all, home-of-all, hearse-of-all night.
آ آ آ آ Her fond yellow hornlight wound to the west, | her wild hollow hoarlight hung to the height
آ آ آ آ Waste; her earliest stars, earl-stars, | stï؟½rs principal, overbend us,
آ آ آ آ Fï؟½re-fï؟½aturing heaven. For earth | her being has unbound, her dapple is at an end, as- tray or aswarm, all throughther, in throngs; | self ï؟½n self steepï؟½d and pashed - qï؟½ite
آ آ آ آ Disremembering, dï؟½smembering | ï؟½ll now. Heart, you round me right
آ آ آ آ With: ï؟½ur ï؟½vening is over us; ï؟½ur night | whï؟½lms, whï؟½lms, ï؟½nd will end us.
آ آ آ آ Only the beak-leaved boughs dragonish | damask the tool-smooth bleak light; black,
آ آ آ آ Ever so black on it. ï؟½ur tale, ï؟½ur oracle! | Lï؟½t life, wï؟½ned, ah lï؟½t life wind
آ آ آ آ Off hï؟½r once skï؟½ined stained vï؟½ined varï؟½ety | upon, ï؟½ll on twï؟½ spools; pï؟½rt, pen, pï؟½ck
آ آ آ آ Now her ï؟½ll in twï؟½ flocks, twï؟½ folds - black, white; | right, wrong; reckon but, reck but, mind
آ آ آ آ But thï؟½se two; wï؟½re of a wï؟½rld where bï؟½t these | twï؟½ tell, each off the ï؟½ther; of a rack
آ آ آ آ Where, selfwrung, selfstrung, sheathe- and shelterless, | thï؟½ughts agaï؟½nst thoughts ï؟½n groans grï؟½nd.
meaning:
"Spelt from Sibyl's Leaves" is a quintessential example of Gerard Manley Hopkins's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
